paper_title: Reconstructing Caribou Seasonal Biogeography Using Intra-Tooth Isotope Analysis

methods.description: This method involves extracting and analyzing strontium and oxygen isotopes from caribou molars to study their seasonal biogeography and migratory behaviors during the Little Ice Age in Western Alaska.

steps.description: Second (M2) and third (M3) molars were extracted from four archaeological caribou hemi-mandibles for isotope analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: extract M2 and M3 molars from caribou mandibles for isotope analysis

steps.description: The molars were selected for strontium and oxygen (carbonate) isotope analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare molars for strontium and oxygen isotope analysis

steps.description: Sequential oxygen and strontium isotope studies of M2 and M3 enamel in modern caribou were used to estimate the time period represented by the molars.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze sequential oxygen and strontium isotopes in molar enamel

methods.method_name: Stable Isotope Analysis for Faunal Migration
methods.description: This method uses stable isotope analysis to reconstruct faunal migration patterns by analyzing isotopic ratios in body tissues, which reflect the isotopic ratios of consumed foods.

steps.description: Initial applications of stable isotope analysis demonstrated that isotopic ratios of foods consumed during life are reflected in the isotopic ratios of body tissues.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze isotopic ratios in body tissues to reflect consumed foods

steps.description: Sequential sampling of incrementally-formed tissues such as enamel, dentine, and antler is used to obtain higher-resolution isotopic data.
steps.searchable_summary: perform sequential sampling of tissues for high-resolution isotopic data

steps.description: Intra-tooth isotopic analysis involves sampling an enamel face horizontally across growth bands to provide time-series isotopic data.
steps.searchable_summary: sample enamel face across growth bands for time-series isotopic data
